Description: Live endpoint confirmed: /api/v3/views/6dca-aqww/query.json with app_token. Default sort is ASC from 2000 — $order DESC mandatory. 2-source fallback chain. Layer 4 now live: net=-9,180 NEUTRAL. COMMODITY_REGIME composite confirmed on all runs since 03-21.

Summary: DEXTER_SYNC_CONTRACT v1.3 — CFTC endpoint + version pairing [MIDAS_v6]

Summary: REGIME_RULES v2.3 — Dollar Decomp wired into weighting [MIDAS_v6]
Description: COMMODITY_REGIME + 4 other composites added to Tier 3 rows and ETL→Regime mapping table. ETL sync 1.7.5.6. Naming fixed.

Summary: SOUL v2.4 — §3.1 Dollar Decomp supplement [MIDAS_v6]
Description: §3.1 Dollar Decomposition added with composite table. ETL filename reference updated. MACRO_TIERS v3.2 xref. Naming fixed.

Summary: MIDAS_SENTINEL_PROMPT_LIBRARY v2026.4 — 3 corrections [MIDAS_v6]
Description: CONVICTION_CAP 0.4 schema added. DOLLAR_DECOMP + BALTIMORE_PSC in checklist. Daily source filenames corrected to ETL 1.7.5.x names. Phase 0 added to TIER_AUDIT integrity table.

Summary: AGENTS.md v1.1 — model, ETL spec, conflict logic, phase order [MIDAS_v6]
Description: Main agent upgraded to gemini-3-flash-preview. ETL spec 1.2.0.0→1.7.5.6. FRA-OIS conflict logic corrected (CB Supremacy). EDGAR web_fetch scope corrected. TIER_AUDIT anchor + CONVICTION_CAP + phase sequence added.

Summary: OPENCLAW_SOUL 2026.3.22 — 6 logic fixes [MIDAS_v6]
Description: Phase scope 1-5→0-5. 40bps Rubicon logic corrected (Regime A elevated, not Regime C). Canonical threshold source: SOUL §3 not SYNC_CONTRACT. DTCC→SEC FTD. Dollar Decomp + CONVICTION_CAP 0.4 + GatePassObject added.

Summary: HEARTBEAT.md v1.1 — 11 fixes [MIDAS_v6]
Description: ETL run at 05:45. NotebookLM upload at 05:55. Phase 0 at 06:00. Phase 0.5 conditional slot. DOLLAR_DECOMP gate check. Phase 5 explicit. DEXTER_SENTINEL→MIDAS_SENTINEL log. 30m FRA-OIS polling removed (SOFR is daily, not real-time). Shadow journal v2026.4 ref.

Summary: Naming standardisation — Dexter ETL / Midas Sentinel [MIDAS_v6]
Description: Dexter = ETL/data layer. Midas Sentinel = analysis/OpenClaw layer. 6 violations fixed across dexter_etl, MACRO_TIERS, REGIME_RULES, MIDAS_SENTINEL manifest. Zero violations remaining.

Summary: dexter_notebooklm_upload.py — automated daily upload [MIDAS_v6]
Description: Uploads all ETL output files + foundation docs to NotebookLM in tier priority order. TIER_AUDIT first. dexter_notebooklm_upload.py using notebooklm-py (teng-lin). --dry-run flag. DEXTER_DATA_DIR + DEXTER_FOUNDATION_DIR env vars.

Summary: Fix cron UTC offset: 45 5 → 10 45 (fires at 01:45 ET, not 05:45 ET) [MIDAS_v6]
Description: Current: 45 5 * * * UTC = 01:45 ET. Target: 10 45 * * 1-5 UTC = 05:45 ET EST. This means the ETL runs in the middle of the night on yesterday's data. Fix before next market day.

Summary: Remove API keys from cron prompt body — move to env vars [MIDAS_v6]
Description: FINNHUB_KEY, FRED_API_KEY, SLACK_WEBHOOK are in plaintext in the cron prompt string. Anyone with cron config access sees all three credentials. Move to Azure VM environment variables or Key Vault. ETL already reads from env vars.

Summary: Fix SLACK_WEBHOOK double-fire — remove from cron prompt body [MIDAS_v6]
Description: SLACK_WEBHOOK in both prompt body and delivery config. ETL already posts its own Slack alert at run end. Remove from prompt body to prevent duplicate messages on every run.

Summary: Add dexter_notebooklm_upload.py as cron step 3 (after ETL, before Phase 0) [MIDAS_v6]
Description: HEARTBEAT 05:55 step. Must run after ETL (which creates the files) and before Phase 0 (which reads them from NotebookLM). All source files must be in notebook before OpenClaw synthesises.

Summary: Add OpenClaw Phase 0-5 as cron step 6 [MIDAS_v6]
Description: Signal Aggregator through GMISR Reporter entirely absent from cron. Phase 0→0.5(conditional)→1→2→3→4→5 must be scheduled after NotebookLM upload completes.

Summary: Add DAILY_STATE.md update as cron step 7 (after Phase 5) [MIDAS_v6]
Description: Phase 5 output feeds DAILY_STATE.md carry-over. Currently not scheduled. Shadow journal section must be filled manually before re-opening NotebookLM.

Summary: Rename feedmd_config_03-01-2026.yml to date-agnostic name [MIDAS_v6]
Description: Hardcoded date in config filename. Will silently use a March 1st config indefinitely. Rename to feedmd_config.yml or derive name dynamically.

Summary: Restrict cron to weekdays (1-5) and confirm weekend intent [MIDAS_v6]
Description: Currently * * * = 7 days/week. Weekend ETL produces stale data. Recommend 1-5 unless Saturday/Sunday runs are intentional. Weekend data could confuse Monday Phase 1 regime read.

Summary: Define DeadLetterEntry dataclass and criticality map [MIDAS_v6]
Description: Typed DeadLetterEntry: timestamp, module, source_name, url, error_type, error_detail, is_critical, retry_hint. Critical sources: FRED WALCL/SOFR, yfinance VIX. Non-critical: Treasury auctions.

Summary: Thread dead-letter through run_fred() and run_treasury() (M1/M2) [MIDAS_v6]
Description: Dead_letters list wired into Modules 1 and 2. Bare except replaced with DeadLetterEntry append.

Summary: Thread dead-letter through run_market() and run_fed_and_dtcc() (M3/M5) [MIDAS_v6]
Description: Dead_letters list wired into Modules 3 and 5.

Summary: Thread dead-letter through run_news() and run_calendars() (M7/M6) [MIDAS_v6]
Description: Two remaining function signatures need dead_letters parameter. run_news() has many feed sources that currently fail silently. run_calendars() Finnhub calls.

Summary: Write FAILED_{date}.json + DATA_FAILURES block in TIER_AUDIT [MIDAS_v6]
Description: After all modules complete: write FAILED_{run_date}.json. In build_tier_audit(): count critical failures, reduce CONVICTION_CAP by 0.2 per critical failure (floor 0.2), append DATA_FAILURES section.

Summary: Define OBSERVATION_RULES dict per module [MIDAS_v6]
Description: Expected minimums: FRED >= 400 chars + NET LIQUIDITY string; Auctions >= 10 records; Market CSV >= 15 rows; EDGAR >= 500 chars + PRIORITY 1; News >= 20000 chars + 8 sources; Calendars >= 100 chars.

Summary: Implement run_offline_observer() as Module 9.5 [MIDAS_v6]
Description: Runs after all modules, before build_tier_audit(). Reads each output file, applies OBSERVATION_RULES, builds observations dict with actual vs expected, pass/fail, degradation level. Cross-references dead-letter list.

Summary: Inject observation results into TIER_AUDIT and completion banner [MIDAS_v6]
Description: Pass observations to build_tier_audit(). Add DATA_QUALITY_REPORT section. CORPUS_QUALITY = (passing/total) × 100. Print in banner alongside CONVICTION_CAP.

Summary: Create staging/ directory structure and write_staged() helper [MIDAS_v6]
Description: Staging subdirectory in dexter_data/YYYYMMDD/. write_staged() writes to staging first; promote() copies to final. Final output files only exist if they passed audit. NotebookLM uploads from root, never sees staging.

Summary: Implement pre-write input audit (Audit step 1) [MIDAS_v6]
Description: audit_input(module, data, rules) → (passed, errors). Per module: FRED all 9 series non-None; market VIX/DGS10/one equity; EDGAR P1 or P2 hit; news 3+ feeds. Fail: write DEGRADED to staging, append dead_letter.

Summary: Implement post-write output audit (Audit step 2) [MIDAS_v6]
Description: audit_output(staged_path, rules). FRED_SUMMARY: all 9 labels; COMMODITIES: WTI+Brent non-zero; TIER_AUDIT: all required sections. Pass → promote(). Fail → AUDIT_FAILED_{module}_{date}.txt.

Summary: Implement three audit failure strategies (Fail/Dispatch/Ignore) [MIDAS_v6]
Description: Per-module AUDIT_FAILURE_STRATEGY: Fail (FRED), Dispatch to dead-letter (Treasury/market), Ignore+DEGRADED (news). TIER_AUDIT reflects which strategy fired.

Summary: Define expected schemas for all external API responses [MIDAS_v6]
Description: SCHEMAS dict in schema_registry.py. Treasury auctions_query required fields. FRED observations list. EDGAR _source with file_date. yfinance DataFrame with Close. Mark required/optional/deprecated.

Summary: Implement validate_schema() and inject into fetch steps [MIDAS_v6]
Description: validate_schema(data, schema) → (compatible, drift_report). Three outcomes: compatible; forward-compatible (new optionals, log); backward-incompatible (required missing, → SCHEMA_DRIFT dead_letter). Inject into run_treasury/fred/edgar/market.

Summary: Write SCHEMA_DRIFT report and auto-update optional field registry [MIDAS_v6]
Description: SCHEMA_DRIFT_{date}.txt when new optionals appear. Auto-learn: new field 3 runs in a row → add to optional list with comment. Backward-incompatible → CRITICAL flag, halt module.

Summary: Define idempotency granularity and document the contract [MIDAS_v6]
Description: run_date (YYYYMMDD) is the idempotency partition key. dexter_data/YYYYMMDD/ is one atomic unit. Two runs for same date produce identical output (same API responses).

Summary: Fix FRED_SUMMARY append bug — most urgent idempotency violation [MIDAS_v6]
Description: Treasury XML block moved before write_file() in run_fred(). Built as tsy_block='', written once as summary+tsy_block. No read_text() anywhere. Confirmed fixed in 1.7.5.2.

Summary: Implement atomic write via stage-then-rename (write_atomic) [MIDAS_v6]
Description: write_atomic(path, content): write to path.with_suffix('.tmp'), then os.replace(tmp→final). Atomic on POSIX. Add startup cleanup of *.tmp orphans. Replaces write_file() everywhere.

Summary: Add run MANIFEST.json with schema version and ETL script hash [MIDAS_v6]
Description: MANIFEST.json in dexter_data/YYYYMMDD/: run_date, etl_version (hash), schema_version, modules_completed, run_start/end. On re-run: skip completed modules unless --force-rerun.

Summary: Make EDGAR incremental with delta-column state file (edgar_state.json) [MIDAS_v6]
Description: State file with last_successful_fetch_dt. since = max(last_fetch, today-14d). After successful write update state. 14-day backfill window. Eliminates ghost-filing problem.

Summary: --force-rerun flag and idempotency test procedure [MIDAS_v6]
Description: CLI flag: delete manifest, clear staging, re-run all. Test: run twice for same date, diff output — byte-identical except TIER_AUDIT timestamp.

Summary: Refactor scrapling_news.py — Category A sources only [MIDAS_v6]
Description: Remove all 10 overlap sources (Reuters/FT/WSJ/CNBC/MarketWatch/BIS/Fed/FRED/SEC/Google — ETL already covers these). Keep: BoJ, PBOC, BoE, BCB, RBI, SAMA, Caixin, Risk.net, Trading Economics, Al Jazeera, Les Echos, Arabian Business, Brazil Journal, Hankook Ilbo, chinanews.com.cn. Reduces runtime ~50% and eliminates redundant data.

Summary: Write merge_scrapling_into_corpus.py — append §SCRAPLING_SUPPLEMENT to NEWS_CORPUS [MIDAS_v6]
Description: Reads scraping output directory, reads existing NEWS_CORPUS_{date}.txt, appends §SCRAPLING_SUPPLEMENT section with tier-tagged content. Must run after ETL (NEWS_CORPUS must exist first) and before NotebookLM upload.

Summary: Document scrapling data lineage in SYNC_CONTRACT v1.4 [MIDAS_v6]
Description: Add scrapling_news.py to source registry. Map Category A sources to MACRO_TIERS tiers: BoJ/PBOC/BoE → Tier -1, Caixin → Tier 2/3, Risk.net → Tier -1/4. Document cron step order dependency.

Summary: Wire scrapling Category A sources into MACRO_TIERS tier routing [MIDAS_v6]
Description: Add BoJ/PBOC/BoE/BCB/RBI to MACRO_TIERS Tier -1 source list. Add Caixin/Brazil Journal to Tier 2/3 HDS routing. Add Risk.net to Tier -1 specialist sources. Update MACRO_TIERS v3.3.

Summary: Add GICS codes to SENTENCE_DIRECTIVE setups (Phase 4 prompt) [MIDAS_v6]
Description: Every driver and active setup must carry full GICS code (e.g. 45103010). Phase 4 prompt addition. No ETL change required.

Summary: Add qualitative invalidation condition per driver [MIDAS_v6]
Description: Each setup in SENTENCE_DIRECTIVE must include: INVALIDATION: [specific condition that cancels the thesis]. e.g. 'US-Iran ceasefire' or 'CEO retracts exit'. Phase 4 prompt addition.

Summary: Add Catalyst Map 24h/48h/72h as Block 10 to Phase 5 GMISR schema [MIDAS_v6]
Description: Structured forward-looking catalyst timeline: what to watch for in next 24h, 48h, 72h. Phase 5 prompt schema addition. Corresponds to v1.0 Block 10.

Summary: Add structured pair trade output to Phase 5 [MIDAS_v6]
Description: Phase 5 GMISR Strategy Matrix block: Long [TICKER] ([GICS]) / Short [TICKER] ([GICS]) with GICS sector logic. Replaces individual setup list. Phase 5 prompt schema addition.

Summary: Add per-claim source citation (Block 11 Data Lineage) to Phase 5 [MIDAS_v6]
Description: Phase 5 GMISR Data Lineage block: cite source per major claim. Financial tables, geopolitical, macro/credit sources named. Phase 5 prompt addition.

Summary: Add NDI (Narrative Divergence Index) to Phase 3 NARRATIVE_ALPHA output [MIDAS_v6]
Description: Count distinct narrative clusters in NEWS_CORPUS. Score 0-1. Add to NARRATIVE_ALPHA contract as NDI field. Phase 3 prompt addition.

Summary: Add Narrative Velocity / NLLI scoring to Phase 3 [MIDAS_v6]
Description: Score velocity of emerging vs established narrative clusters. New cluster gaining saturation = positive NLLI. Add NLLI to NARRATIVE_ALPHA contract.

Summary: Add Claude (Anthropic API) as Lineage B agent in AGENTS.md [MIDAS_v6]
Description: Add second researcher agent using Claude Sonnet via Anthropic API. Lineage A = Gemini flash-preview, Lineage B = Claude Sonnet. Both run Phase 3 (Fundamental Agent) independently on same corpus.

Summary: Write Phase 3.5 delta synthesiser prompt [MIDAS_v6]
Description: New phase between Phase 3 and Phase 4. Takes both NARRATIVE_ALPHA contracts (Lineage A + B). Identifies agreements (high confidence), disagreements (flag for human review), and synthesises into a single consolidated NARRATIVE_ALPHA_CONSENSUS. When lineages disagree on direction: flag CONFLICT_WITH_LINEAGE_B = TRUE.

Summary: Add Model Delta block (Block 4) to Phase 5 GMISR schema [MIDAS_v6]
Description: Block 4 in GMISR: what did Lineage A say, what did Lineage B say, where did they diverge, what was the synthesis decision. Maps to v1.0 Block 4.

Summary: Define sizing rule from lineage delta [MIDAS_v6]
Description: When Lineage A and B disagree on direction: use cautionary lineage for position sizing (per v1.0 logic: Claude divergence for pair selection, ChatGPT cautionary sizing). Encode in Phase 4 SENTENCE_DIRECTIVE sizing formula.

Summary: Implement HDS auto-scoring in run_news() [MIDAS_v6]
Description: Compute Headline Density Score per tier: count of matching headlines per tier_keywords group / total items. Write HDS per tier to NEWS_CORPUS header section. Top 3 themes by count = blob detection.

Summary: Implement VPI (Volatility Pricing Index) in build_tier_audit() [MIDAS_v6]
Description: VPI = VIX_spot / (5-day realized vol proxy). VIX > realized → VIX lagging = VPI > 1 (underpriced stress). Requires 5-day VIX history from RUN_HISTORY.log. Add to TIER_AUDIT.

Summary: Implement LFI (Liquidity Friction Index) in build_tier_audit() [MIDAS_v6]
Description: LFI = composite of: FRA-OIS normalised (0-1 over 0-60bps range) + BDC price discount to NAV proxy + EDGAR 8-K withdrawal/redemption count. Write to TIER_AUDIT.

Summary: Implement NLLI (Narrative Lag-Lead Indicator) in run_news() [MIDAS_v6]
Description: Compare current run's tier headline counts to prior 5-run rolling average. Rising count in tier = positive NLLI for that tier. Write NLLI vector to NEWS_CORPUS header.

Summary: Implement CCS (Cross-Correlation Score) in run_market() [MIDAS_v6]
Description: CCS = correlation between ratio-pair movements (TQQQ/SQQQ, Gold/NASDAQ etc) vs broad index. Low CCS = idiosyncratic alpha opportunity. Requires 5-day price history.

Summary: Implement CXOD (Cross-Asset Order Delta) in run_market() [MIDAS_v6]
Description: CXOD = signed flow divergence: (USO 1d return) - (QQQ 1d return). Positive = energy bid, equities sold. Negative = risk-on. Write to COMMODITIES/EQUITIES files.

Summary: Implement PSI (Policy Sensitivity Index) in build_tier_audit() [MIDAS_v6]
Description: PSI = composite of: GDP revision magnitude + FRA-OIS level + high-impact events within 5 days + earnings guidance revision count. Higher PSI = higher policy error risk.

Summary: COT/CFTC positioning (Module 6b Layer 4) — LIVE [MIDAS_v6]
Description: CFTC non-commercial net long on USD Index (098662) via /api/v3/. Confirmed working 2026-03-22. net=-9,180 = NEUTRAL. Composite unaffected by positioning at this level.

Summary: Gold/NASDAQ absolute >0.30 flag in TIER_AUDIT [MIDAS_v6]
Description: MA deviation exists (gn_ma_pct). Absolute ratio flag (gn_ratio > 300 at ×1000 scale) is missing from TIER_AUDIT. Add as separate row in TIER_5 block.

Summary: RATIO_SIGNALS named section in TIER_AUDIT — schema 2.0.0.0 [MIDAS_v6]
Description: Major schema change: add dedicated RATIO_SIGNALS section listing all SOUL §3 ratio pair statuses in a consistent format. Requires build_tier_audit() rewrite and version bump.

Summary: Add write_parquet_snapshot() to ETL main() [MIDAS_v6]
Description: After build_tier_audit() completes, write one Parquet row to dexter_data/parquet/gmisr_metrics/ partitioned by run_date. 28 typed columns: all TIER_AUDIT scalars + scored metrics (HDS/VPI/LFI/NLLI/PSI/CXOD/NDI) + regime + conviction_cap + sentence_bias + dollar_composite + cftc_net + all ratio values. pandas .to_parquet(). Zero infra — writes to local disk. ETL bump to 1.8.0.0.

Summary: Define and lock GMISR_METRICS Parquet schema v1.0 [MIDAS_v6]
Description: Create schema_registry.py entry for gmisr_metrics. 28 typed columns. Scored metric columns NULLABLE until SCORE-1/7 Wave 5 work lands. Schema version in MANIFEST.json. Breaking changes require migration script.

Summary: Add phase1_agreement and actual_spx_5d ML label columns [MIDAS_v6]
Description: phase1_agreement BOOL: written retrospectively from DAILY_STATE.md shadow journal field after Phase 10. actual_spx_5d FLOAT: forward-filled weekly cron — SPX return for runs where 5 trading days elapsed. These are the two primary ML target label columns. Cannot be filled at run time.

Summary: Write backfill_parquet.py — populate history from existing run archives [MIDAS_v6]
Description: One-time script: reads RUN_HISTORY.log to enumerate past run dates, parses each dexter_data/YYYYMMDD/ TIER_AUDIT + FRED_SUMMARY + DOLLAR_DECOMP into structured dicts, writes Parquet rows for all historical dates. Nullable for fields not in older ETL versions. Idempotent: skips run_dates already in store.

Summary: Set up DuckDB + Evidence.dev local dashboard [MIDAS_v6]
Description: pip install duckdb. Write dexter_analytics.py: connects to gmisr_metrics Parquet, exposes 5 standard queries (regime trend 90d, metric heatmap, conviction history, NLLI velocity chart, phase1_agreement accuracy rate). Evidence.dev renders static local dashboard from SQL. Zero infra, runs on-machine.

Summary: Build 30/60/90-day regime and metrics dashboard [MIDAS_v6]
Description: Dashboard pages: (1) Regime timeline bar chart with conviction_cap overlay. (2) Scored metrics heatmap HDS/VPI/LFI/NLLI/PSI colour-coded by week. (3) Dollar decomp history COMMODITY_REGIME vs TRUE_RISK_OFF over time. (4) Shadow journal accuracy — rolling PHASE1_AGREEMENT % by month. Replaces ad-hoc NotebookLM lookbacks.

Summary: Implement Feast feature store — point-in-time correct training sets [MIDAS_v6]
Description: pip install feast. Define FeatureView over gmisr_metrics Parquet. Entity = run_date. Features = all scored metrics + tier fields. Materialise to local SQLite online store. Key function: get_historical_features() returns training dataset with no look-ahead bias. Prerequisite for Wave 7 HMM classifier.

Summary: Build regime classifier v1.0 — HMM on scored metrics [MIDAS_v6]
Description: Requires >= 30 labelled run_dates (Kelly activation gate). Features: [HDS, VPI, LFI, VX_ratio, FRA-OIS, net_liq_roc, NLLI]. Label: regime A/B/C. Model: Hidden Markov Model (hmmlearn). Regime = hidden state, metrics = observations. Validation: HMM calls vs PHASE1_AGREEMENT shadow journal labels. Phase 10 auditor reports HMM accuracy weekly. Do not activate before 30-trade gate.

Summary: Build SENTENCE_DIRECTIVE outcome tracker — P&L attribution log [MIDAS_v6]
Description: For each SENTENCE_DIRECTIVE conviction >= 0.40: record entry trigger, stop, target, actual_exit, outcome (win/loss/stopped/expired). Write to trade_log.parquet partitioned by run_date. Phase 10 auditor reads trade_log for attribution. Kelly fraction activates when trade_log has >= 30 closed trades.

Summary: Kelly fraction activation and SOUL.md v3.0 update [MIDAS_v6]
Description: When trade_log >= 30 closed: compute win_rate and avg_win/loss_ratio. Kelly = win_rate - (1-win_rate)/ratio. Phase 10 produces Kelly note for SOUL.md. Fixed-fractional (1%) active until this gate. Transition from paper-sizing to empirical sizing — SOUL.md version 3.0.

Summary: Enrich run_calendars() — add region, status (released/upcoming), ET time conversion [MIDAS_v6]
Description: Current: filters HIGH only, no country code, UTC time, no released/upcoming split. Add: region lookup dict mapping event strings → country code (US/EU/JP/UK/EM). Add: status = RELEASED if event_time < run_time else UPCOMING. Add: ET timezone conversion (UTC → America/New_York). Add: medium-impact events (currently dropped). Produces richer ECON_CALENDAR_{date}.txt.

Summary: Add ForexFactory / TradingEconomics calendar as primary source alongside Finnhub [MIDAS_v6]
Description: ForexFactory XML calendar (forexfactory.com/calendar.xml) is the de facto FX trader standard. Provides: country, impact (Low/Medium/High), title, forecast, actual, previous. Free, no auth. TradingEconomics RSS as secondary. Merge with Finnhub, deduplicate by event+date. ForexFactory covers ECB speaker events, central bank speeches Finnhub misses.

Summary: Add Atlanta Fed GDPNow as a dedicated ETL source (Module 6c) [MIDAS_v6]
Description: GDPNow updates Tuesday and Thursday at ~13:00 ET. Scrape atlantafed.org/cqer/research/gdpnow current estimate + prior estimate. Write to ECON_CALENDAR_{date}.txt as a separate NOWCAST section. Tier 1 relevance: GDP nowcast is a leading indicator of Fed policy path. Uses scrapling (JS-rendered page) or direct HTML parse.

Summary: Add tier_tag mapping to each calendar event [MIDAS_v6]
Description: Create ECON_TIER_MAP dict: keywords → tier. Examples: 'CPI','PCE','inflation' → Tier 2; 'GDP','NFP','unemployment' → Tier 1; 'fed funds','FOMC','rate decision' → Tier -1; 'BoJ','ECB','BoE' speaker → Tier -1 + Tier 3; trade balance → Tier 3. Write tier_tag to each event line in ECON_CALENDAR. Phase 0 uses this to route event into the correct tier section of TIER_AUDIT.

Summary: Compute and write event suppression scalars per-event in ECON_CALENDAR [MIDAS_v6]
Description: Current: suppression scalar note is text-only. Add per-event scalar computation: FOMC day = 0.25, FOMC T-1 = 0.50, CPI day = 0.30, CPI T-1 = 0.60, Major CB speaker (ECB/BoE/BoJ) = 0.75, Major auction day = 0.75, no events = 1.0. Write both scalar and next-day T-1 scalar to file. Phase 2 GatePass reads these directly instead of re-computing from raw event list.

Summary: Add released vs upcoming split with actual vs forecast delta in ECON_CALENDAR [MIDAS_v6]
Description: For RELEASED events (event_time < run_time): compute surprise_delta = actual - forecast. Flag BEAT (delta positive, lower=better reversed for CPI etc) or MISS. Write: 'RELEASED -- Singapore CPI 1.2% actual vs 1.3% forecast [BEAT] [TIER 2]'. For UPCOMING events: write forecast, prior, time until release. Phase 3 Fundamental Agent reads RELEASED actuals as a tier-tagged signal source.

Summary: Add central bank speaker event scraping from ECB/BoJ/BoE press release RSS [MIDAS_v6]
Description: ECB: ecb.europa.eu/press/calendars/mopo/html/index.en.html RSS. BoJ: boj.or.jp/en/announcements/release/index.htm RSS. BoE: bankofengland.co.uk/rss/publications RSS. Extract speaker name, title, time, topic. Write as SPEAKER_EVENTS section in ECON_CALENDAR. Tier tag: Tier -1 (rate path) + Tier 3 (FX impact). These are the events Finnhub misses (ECB Lane example in feedback).

Summary: Add Block 2.5 Economic Calendar to Phase 5 GMISR schema [MIDAS_v6]
Description: New block between tier snapshot (Block 3) and scored metrics (Block 2). Structure: (1) Released section with actual/forecast/prior and tier-tagged narrative. (2) Upcoming section with suppression scalar per event. (3) Suppression scalar summary line for Phase 2 gate reference. Region and impact filter buttons in HTML output. Phase 3 Fundamental Agent generates 1-sentence narrative per high/med-impact event using the tier_tag and surprise_delta fields from ECON_CALENDAR.

Summary: Add filing size, CIK, and minute-level timestamp to EDGAR_.txt output [MIDAS_v6]
Description: Current: entity name + form type + date only. Add: _id (CIK), file_size_bytes (from EDGAR EFTS _source.file_size or HEAD request), and filed_at timestamp with HH:MM. Size is a primary v2.0 signal: 301KB CoreWeave cluster vs 10KB routine Form 4. Minute-level timing enables window clustering (7-min CoreWeave, 9-min NVIDIA, 1-min Cadence). Output format: DATE HH:MM  FORM  ENTITY  CIK  SIZE_KB  SIGNAL_NOTES

Summary: Add Form 4 transaction type parsing — acquisition vs disposal direction [MIDAS_v6]
Description: Current: all Form 4s treated equally. Need: parse transaction code from Form 4 XML (transactionCode field: P=purchase, S=sale, F=tax withholding, A=award, M=option exercise). Only P (open-market purchase) and S (open-market sale) are credible signals. Filter out: F, A, M, V (option exercises, awards, RSU vesting). Write direction to EDGAR_.txt as [BUY] or [SELL] or [RSU/OPTION - not signal]. Requires EDGAR individual filing XML parse for each Form 4 hit.

Summary: Add GICS 8-digit code lookup from SIC code on all filing entities [MIDAS_v6]
Description: EDGAR EFTS returns sic (SIC code) in _source. Build SIC_TO_GICS mapping dict (SIC 3674=45301020 Semiconductors, SIC 7372=45103010 Application Software, etc). Approximately 200 mappings cover 90% of filing volume. Write GICS code to every filing row in EDGAR_.txt. Enables: buy/sell ratio aggregation by 8-digit sector, GICS divergence analysis (v2.0 §2C), and validated pair trade GICS in SENTENCE_DIRECTIVE.

Summary: Add officer level filter to Form 4 — CEO/CFO/COO/President only for credibility filter [MIDAS_v6]
Description: EDGAR Form 4 XML contains reportingOwner/officerTitle field. Parse: flag CEO, CFO, COO, President, EVP as OFFICER_LEVEL_PASS. Flag: VP, Director, 10% Owner as OFFICER_LEVEL_WATCH. Flag: Board member as OFFICER_LEVEL_ROUTINE (lower signal). Write officer level to EDGAR_.txt. Enables v2.0 credibility filter: 'CEO/CFO/COO open-market buys/sells >$500K = PASS'. Requires Form 4 XML parse per filing (same call as SEC-2).

Summary: Add minute-window cluster detection for same-entity or same-sector filing bursts [MIDAS_v6]
Description: Current: same-day cluster only. Add: 15-minute window cluster. Algorithm: sort all Form 4s by filed_at timestamp. Slide 15-minute window. If 3+ filings against same entity OR same GICS sector within window: flag as TIMED_CLUSTER with window duration (e.g. '4 entities · 7 min · 301KB'). TIMED_CLUSTER > SAME_DAY_CLUSTER in signal strength. Write cluster flag prominently at top of P1 section.

Summary: Add Form 144 confirmation tracking — cross-run state for proposed vs confirmed sales [MIDAS_v6]
Description: Form 144 = proposed sale only. v2.0 constraint: do not use as short confirmation until corresponding Form 4 disposal appears under same CIK. Implement: write pending_144s.json to dexter_data/ — dict of {CIK: [proposed_date, entity]}. On each run: check pending_144s.json against today's Form 4 disposals. If Form 4 disposal found for same CIK: mark as CONFIRMED, clear from pending. Write 144 status to EDGAR_.txt: [PROPOSED - awaiting Form 4] or [CONFIRMED - Form 4 filed DATE].

Summary: Add Item 7.01 Reg FD mandatory extraction from 8-K filings [MIDAS_v6]
Description: Current: 8-K keyword search only. Add: specific Item 7.01 extraction. EDGAR EFTS search for 8-K with q='Item 7.01' OR 'Regulation FD'. For each hit, fetch the filing index page to identify exhibit type. Write to EDGAR_.txt: 'Item 7.01 Reg FD: [entity] [date] — [what was selectively disclosed]'. Phase 3 LLM instruction: Item 7.01 is mandatory extraction — the signal is what management shared with analysts before public filing.

Summary: Add 13D→13G and 13G→13D reclassification detection [MIDAS_v6]
Description: Current: retrieves both form types but does not detect direction changes. Add: for each 13D/G amendment, check prior filing by same filer+CIK in edgar_state.json. If prior = 13D and current = 13G: flag 13D→13G HARVEST (activist reducing, often precedes exit). If prior = 13G and current = 13D: flag 13G→13D DEPLOYMENT (passive becoming activist). Write reclassification direction to EDGAR_.txt: '[HARVEST] Filer X: 13D→13G on Entity Y — activist reducing influence'. State: harvest/deployment is more important than the raw form type.

Summary: Upgrade EDGAR LLM instruction block to v2.0 prompt schema [MIDAS_v6]
Description: Replace current 6-line Phase 3 instruction block with full v2.0 schema. Add: (1) Blind Spot section — naming the one truth the news cycle cannot see. (2) Assumption Invalidation — which consensus assumption today's filings challenge. (3) Alpha Signal with all required v2.0 fields: GICS 8-digit, Signal Source with item number, Textual Evidence citation, Thesis, NLLI Phase (Velocity/Exhaustion), Entry Trigger, Time Horizon, LFI/CCS/CXOD qualifiers, 144 Confirmation Status, Key Risk, Invalidation Signal. (4) Chain-of-thought enforcement: no inference without filing citation.

Summary: Add SEC Filing Analysis Prompt v2.0 as a foundation file in NotebookLM upload [MIDAS_v6]
Description: SEC_Filing_Analysis_Prompt_Version_2_0.md uploaded to NotebookLM alongside SOUL.md / MACRO_TIERS.md etc. Phase 3 uses it as the forensic framework document. Add to dexter_notebooklm_upload.py FOUNDATION_FILES list. Add to HEARTBEAT.md 06:00 upload checklist. Add to MIDAS_SENTINEL_PROMPT_LIBRARY v2026.5 Phase 3 source document reference.

Summary: Add Block 5 EDGAR Forensic to Phase 5 GMISR schema with v2.0 structure [MIDAS_v6]
Description: New dedicated EDGAR block in Phase 5 GMISR output. Structure: (1) Cluster alert section — timed clusters at top with entity/window/size metadata. (2) Filing inventory table with Form | Entity | GICS | CIK | Size | Direction | NLLI Phase. (3) Blind Spot — one truth news cycle cannot see (with filing citation). (4) Assumption Invalidation — consensus challenged by today's filings. (5) Alpha Signal pair trade with full v2.0 fields including LFI/CCS/CXOD qualifiers and Form 144 confirmation status. P1-P5 filter buttons in HTML output (clusters vs routine toggle). Phase 3 NARRATIVE_ALPHA contract adds: blind_spot, assumption_invalidation, edgar_nlli_phase fields.

Summary: Install py-clob-client and write run_polymarket() — Module 10 [MIDAS_v6]
Description: pip install py-clob-client (official Polymarket Python SDK). New function run_polymarket(out_dir, run_date, dead_letters). Query Gamma API (gamma-api.polymarket.com/markets) — no auth required for read-only. Level 0 (no wallet) sufficient for midpoint price queries. Fetch YES token midpoint price for each watched slug → implied probability (0–1 = 0–100%). Graceful failure: if Gamma API unreachable, write UNAVAILABLE and continue pipeline. ETL version bump to 1.8.1.0.

Summary: Define POLY_WATCH_SLUGS — 7 macro event slugs mapped to MACRO_TIERS tiers [MIDAS_v6]
Description: Create module-level constant POLY_WATCH_SLUGS dict: slug → (display_name, tier_tag, soul_threshold_reference). Initial 7 slugs: fed-rate-cut-2026 (Tier -1, fra_ois_watch), fomc-pause-may (Tier -1, fra_ois_emergency), us-recession-2026 (Tier -2, net_liq_roc_emergency), brent-crude-above-100-june (Tier 2, COMMODITY_REGIME), us-cpi-above-4-percent-2026 (Tier 1, T10YIE), japan-rate-hike-q2-2026 (Tier 3, usdjpy_deleverage), ecb-rate-cut-june-2026 (Tier 3, usdchf_stress). Slugs verified against live Gamma API before committing. Document in SYNC_CONTRACT v1.4 as new data source.

Summary: Compute 24h probability delta from Parquet history and write POLYMARKET_SNAPSHOT file [MIDAS_v6]
Description: Load prior day's polymarket_signals Parquet row (from parquet store, P1-1 prerequisite). Compute delta_24h = current_prob - prior_prob for each slug. Flag ALERT if abs(delta_24h) >= 0.05 (5 percentage point move in 24h). Write POLYMARKET_SNAPSHOT_{date}.txt — structured format: SLUG | PROB_PCT | DELTA_24H | LIQUIDITY_USD | TIER_TAG | ALERT | SIGNAL_NOTE. SIGNAL_NOTE is one sentence: 'brent-above-100 at 78% and rising (+6pp) — aligns with COMMODITY_REGIME composite active in ETL TIER_3.' Also write row to polymarket_signals Parquet table partitioned by run_date.

Summary: Add liquidity filter — minimum $50K open interest to suppress noise [MIDAS_v6]
Description: Polymarket markets vary from $1K to $100M+ in liquidity. Low-liquidity markets (<$50K) produce volatile prices that do not represent genuine information aggregation — they are manipulable by single traders. Add: query Gamma API market volume/liquidity fields. If market volume_24h < $50,000: write ILLIQUID flag, reduce signal confidence to LOW. If volume_24h >= $50,000 and < $500,000: MEDIUM confidence. If volume_24h >= $500,000: HIGH confidence. Write confidence level to POLYMARKET_SNAPSHOT alongside each slug.

Summary: Add oracle dispute risk flag based on event type classification [MIDAS_v6]
Description: Six major Polymarket controversies in 2025 exhibited recurring failure modes: definition ambiguity, oracle disputes, settlement refusals. Add ORACLE_RISK classification per slug type: LOW — binary economic data releases (CPI above/below X%): objective, numeric settlement. MEDIUM — Fed/ECB rate decisions: objective but timing disputes possible. HIGH — geopolitical/conflict events: definition disputes common (Hormuz 'closed' definition). Write ORACLE_RISK to POLYMARKET_SNAPSHOT. Phase 3 instruction: do not use HIGH oracle_risk slugs as ground truth — directional signal only with stated confidence weighting.

Summary: Persist polymarket_signals to Parquet — Layer 1 integration [MIDAS_v6]
Description: Add polymarket_signals table to gmisr_metrics Parquet store (Wave 6 P1-1 prerequisite). Schema: run_date DATE PK, slug VARCHAR, prob_pct FLOAT, delta_24h FLOAT, volume_usd FLOAT, confidence VARCHAR, oracle_risk VARCHAR, alert BOOL. One row per slug per day. Enables: DuckDB queries across 90 days of probability history, correlation with regime labels, feature for Wave 7 HMM classifier. Add POLYMARKET_SNAPSHOT_{date}.txt to NotebookLM upload list in dexter_notebooklm_upload.py DAILY_FILES_PRIORITY.

Summary: Add polymarket_divergence field to Phase 3 NARRATIVE_ALPHA contract [MIDAS_v6]
Description: Phase 3 NARRATIVE_ALPHA contract new field: polymarket_divergence. Phase 3 instruction: for each POLY_WATCH_SLUG with confidence >= MEDIUM: (1) Compare implied probability against current TIER_AUDIT regime read. (2) Check alignment: brent-above-100 at 78% + COMMODITY_REGIME = ALIGNED.     fed-rate-cut at 67% + Regime A (contraction) = CONTRADICTS. (3) If CONTRADICTS: surface as polymarket_divergence signal —     'Polymarket implies [X] but ETL regime implies [Y] — divergence is the signal.' (4) Do not resolve the contradiction — surface it for human review. CONFIDENCE WEIGHTING: Polymarket < SEC filings < TIER_AUDIT. If Polymarket and TIER_AUDIT diverge, never override TIER_AUDIT.

Summary: Add Block 2.7 Prediction Market Probability Layer to Phase 5 GMISR schema [MIDAS_v6]
Description: New block between Economic Calendar (2.5) and Scored Metrics (2). Structure: (1) Probability table: SLUG | PROB | 24H_DELTA | CONFIDENCE | ALIGNMENT.     Alignment = ALIGNED / PARTIAL / CONTRADICTS vs current ETL regime.     Delta coloured: green=prob rising toward regime thesis, red=falling. (2) Divergence alert: if any slug CONTRADICTS current regime —     surface as named alert: 'Polymarket fed-rate-cut at 67% contradicts     Regime A (contraction) — smart money pricing pivot not yet in ETL signal.' (3) Confirmation note: slugs that ALIGN with active regime add confidence.     'brent-above-100 at 78% (+6pp) confirms COMMODITY_REGIME composite.' Filter buttons: All / Macro / Energy / FX / Geopolitical. Confidence and oracle_risk badges per slug.

Summary: Update DEXTER_SYNC_CONTRACT v1.4 — Polymarket source registry [MIDAS_v6]
Description: Add Polymarket section to SYNC_CONTRACT: API: gamma-api.polymarket.com (no auth, read-only) — confirmed working. SDK: py-clob-client (pip install py-clob-client). Auth: Level 0 (no wallet) for read queries. Cadence: daily at ETL run time (05:45 ET). Output: POLYMARKET_SNAPSHOT_{date}.txt. Parquet: polymarket_signals table. Confidence hierarchy: SEC EDGAR > TIER_AUDIT > Polymarket > RSS. Oracle risk taxonomy: LOW (numeric release) / MEDIUM (rate decision) / HIGH (geopolitical). Conflict resolution: TIER_AUDIT always wins. Divergence = surface to human, not auto-resolve. DO NOT use: poly_data library, Apify scraper, 30-minute intraday monitor (wrong architecture for daily brief pipeline).

Summary: Add Polymarket to MACRO_TIERS v3.3 as a Tier -1 / Tier 3 leading indicator source [MIDAS_v6]
Description: Add to MACRO_TIERS §Tier -1 (Repo / Interbank / CCBS): 'Polymarket prediction markets for Fed/ECB/BoJ rate decisions provide real-time implied probability of rate path changes. HIGH liquidity (>$500K) and LOW oracle_risk slugs only. Treat as directional signal below TIER_AUDIT confidence.' Add to §Tier 3 (FX): 'Polymarket geopolitical slugs (Hormuz, conflict escalation) provide leading probability for DXY safe-haven demand spikes. HIGH oracle_risk — use with explicit caveat.' Version bump: MACRO_TIERS v3.3.

Summary: Add earnings volatility contract monitoring for Tier 6 earnings Delta cross-check [MIDAS_v6]
Description: For active earnings week: query Polymarket for earnings-related contracts (e.g. ADBE-earnings-beat, NVDA-revenue-above-X). Cross-reference with Tier 6 Earnings Delta score from EARNINGS_CALENDAR. Phase 3 instruction: 'Compare implied earnings beat probability from Polymarket with Earnings Delta score from EDGAR and management tone from NARRATIVE_ALPHA. Is there a divergence between prediction market and fundamental signal?' Only for HIGH liquidity contracts (>$500K). Flag LOW/MEDIUM liquidity as noise. Add to EARNINGS_CALENDAR section of GMISR as supplemental probability field.

Summary: SOUL.md §2 model output epistemic guardrail — [MODEL_BELIEF] label [MIDAS_v6]
Description: Add §2 clause: any output from BISTRO/MOIRAI in GMISR must be labeled [MODEL_BELIEF]. CONDITIONAL_CORRELATION not CAUSAL_INFERENCE. BIS paper explicitly warns: BISTRO learns correlation patterns, not structural mechanisms. Zero Hallucination Policy extension. SOUL v2.3 → v2.4+. Doc-only change, no code.

Summary: BISTRO/MOIRAI Colab Track B — start shadow pipeline in paper mode [MIDAS_v6]
Description: Start today. Clone bis-med-it/bistro + pip install uni2ts. Load ETL FRED CSVs as y. Load NEWS_CORPUS headlines as SBERT covariates (PCA-3 dims). Run baseline forecast + FRA-OIS stress scenario (25bps acceleration). Log TRACK_B_* fields in DAILY_STATE.md. Reference: BISTRO_MOIRAI_CHEATSHEET.md. No ETL code change required. Convergence clock starts on first run. 20-day paper mode before promotion to ETL module.

Summary: Promote Track B → ETL module: bayesian_regime_filter.py after 20-day validation [MIDAS_v6]
Description: After 20 trading days of Colab Track B with documented TRACK_B_REGIME_IMPLICATION vs REGIME_LABEL comparison: promote MOIRAI-based {P_A, P_B, P_C} to ETL module. Classification head on MOIRAI quantile output. Hamilton Switching Model or HMM. Prerequisite: IQ-1a (FRA-OIS Δ1/Δ2) and IQ-3b (FinBERT embeddings) as inputs. ETL version 2.3.0.0. Convergence criterion must be documented in SOUL.md §6 before promotion.

Summary: Upgrade run_news() keyword tagging → FinBERT/SBERT neural embeddings [MIDAS_v6]
Description: Replace keyword match (misses semantic content — "Hormuz insurance premiums" tags UNCLASSIFIED) with all-MiniLM-L6-v2 (80MB, ~1s/headline) or ProsusAI/finbert. Same tier-tagging interface, dramatically improved recall. Standalone BUILD — no schema change. Improves Track B covariate quality immediately. Reference: BISTRO_MOIRAI_CHEATSHEET.md §3 and §7.
